name: Nat

aliases.name: Nat
aliases.comment:
A **bounded** natural number (`>= 0`).

    -- >= 4
    Nat (Min Nat4)

    -- 2 <= nat <= 12
    Nat (In Nat2 Nat12)

    -- = 3
    Nat (Only Nat3)

    -- = 3, & 3, described as a difference
    Nat (N Nat3 Is (Difference a To (Nat3Plus a)))

    -- either one of the above
    Nat range

    -- Not constuctable
    Nat Banana

values.name: theGreater
values.comment:
The greater of 2 `Nat`s. Works just like `Basics.max`.

    Nat.theGreater
        (nat3 |> Nat.N.toMin)
        (nat4 |> Nat.N.toMin |> Nat.Min.lowerMin (nat3 |> Nat.N.toIn))
    --> Nat 4
values.type: Nat.Nat range -> Nat.Nat range -> Nat.Nat range

values.name: theSmaller
values.comment:
The smaller of 2 `Nat`s. Works just like `Basics.min`.

    Nat.theSmaller
        (nat3 |> Nat.N.toMin)
        (nat4 |> Nat.N.toMin |> Nat.Min.lowerMin (nat3 |> Nat.N.toIn))
    --> Nat 3
values.type: Nat.Nat range -> Nat.Nat range -> Nat.Nat range

values.name: toInt
values.comment:
Convert a `Nat` to an `Int`.

    nat4 |> Nat.toInt --> 4
    Nat.N.toIn nat4 |> Nat.toInt --> 4
    Nat.N.toMin nat4 |> Nat.toInt --> 4

    compare a b =
        compare (Nat.toInt a) (Nat.toInt b)
values.type: Nat.Nat range -> Basics.Int

name: Nat.Bound

unions.name: Difference
unions.comment:
`b - a`.

    Difference a To (Nat1Plus a)

would describe a difference of 1.

unions.name: In
unions.comment:
`In minimum maximum`: Somewhere within a minimum & maximum.

       ↓ minimum   ↓ maximum
    ⨯ [✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓] ⨯ ⨯ ⨯...

Note: maximum >= minimum for every existing `Nat (In ...)`:

    percent : Nat (In minimum Nat100)

→ `minimum <= Nat100`

unions.name: Is
unions.comment:
No special meaning.

    N n Is difference

unions.name: Min
unions.comment:
`Min minimum`: At least `minimum`.

       ↓ minimum
    ⨯ [✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓...

Any natural number:

    Nat (Min Nat0)

A number, at least 5:

    Nat (Min Nat5)

unions.name: N
unions.comment:
The _actual value_ is present in the type.

Looking at the type

    nat0 :
        -- natural number
        Nat
            (N
                -- 0 is the exact value at compile time
                Nat0
                Is
                -- 0 as a difference is a - a
                (Difference a To a)
            )

It is also used to describe a difference between two values.

    interval :
        { first : Nat (N first Is (Difference range To last))
        , last : Nat (Only last)
        }
        -> Interval

→ because `range` is 0 or positive, `last` must also be at least as high as `first`.

If you only want to ensure that it is within a minimum (& maximum), [`Min`](Nat-Bound#Min) or [`In`](Nat-Bound#In) is the right choice!
This is most of the time the better choice for calculations.

unions.name: To
unions.comment:
No special meaning.

    Difference a To b

aliases.name: Only
aliases.comment:
Just the exact number.

Only useful in function annotations.

    byte : Arr (Only Nat8) Bit -> Byte

→ A given [`Arr`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/indique/elm-bounded-array/latest/) must have _exact 8_ `Bit`s.

    repeat : Nat (Only n) -> element -> Arr (Only n)

    repeat (Nat.N.toIn nat6) "ok"
    --> Arr (Only Nat6)

→ repeating an `element` `n` times will give you a Arr of `n` elements.

→ Every `In NatXYZ (NatXYZPlus a)` is also a `Only NatXYZ`, _but not the other way around_

name: Nat.In

values.name: add
values.comment:
Add a `Nat (In ...)`.

    Nat.N.toIn nat3
        |> Nat.In.add between1And12 nat1 nat12
    --> of type Nat (In Nat4 (Nat15Plus a))
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In addedMin addedMax) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N addedMin Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference min Nat.Bound.To sumMin)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N addedMax Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference max Nat.Bound.To sumMax)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In sumMin sumMax)

values.name: addN
values.comment:
Add a fixed `Nat (N ...)` value.

    Nat.N.toIn nat70
        |> Nat.In.addN ( nat7, nat7 )
    --> is of type Nat (In Nat77 (Nat77Plus a))
values.type: ( Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N added Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference min Nat.Bound.To sumMin)), Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N added Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference max Nat.Bound.To sumMax)) ) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In sumMin sumMax)

values.name: atLeast
values.comment:
If the `Nat (In ...)` is less than a number, return that number instead.

    nat5 |> Nat.N.toIn
        |> Nat.In.atLeast nat10
    --> Nat 10

    nat15 |> Nat.N.toIn
        |> Nat.In.atLeast (nat10 |> Nat.N.toIn)
    --> Nat 15
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In newMin max) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In newMin max)

values.name: atMost
values.comment:
**Cap** the `Nat (In ...)` to at most a number.

    nat5 |> Nat.N.toIn
        |> Nat.In.atMost nat10 { min = nat5 }
    --> Nat.In 5

    nat15 |> Nat.N.toIn
        |> Nat.In.lowerMin (nat5 |> Nat.N.toIn)
        |> Nat.In.atMost nat10 { min = nat5 }
    --> Nat 10

`min` ensures that that number is at least the minimum.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N newMax Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference a Nat.Bound.To newMaxPlusA)) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference newRange Nat.Bound.To newMax)) } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min oldMax) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min newMaxPlusA)

values.name: div
values.comment:
Divide (`//`) by a `Nat (Min ...)`. `div 0` is impossible.

    Nat.N.toIn nat7 |> Nat.In.div (nat3 |> Nat.N.toMin)
    --> Nat 2 of type Nat (In Nat0 (Nat7Plus a))
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus dMinMinus1)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In Nat.N.Type.Nat0 max)

values.name: dropMax
values.comment:
Convert a `Nat (In ...)` to a `Nat (Min ...)`.

    squareAInNat =
        Nat.In.dropMax >> Nat.Min.toPower (nat2 |> Nat.N.toMin)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min)

values.name: intInRange
values.comment:
A `Nat (In ...)` from an `Int`, **clamped** between a minimum & maximum.

  - if the `Int < minimum`, `minimum` is returned
  - if the `Int > maximum`, `maximum` is returned

```
clampBetween3And12 =
    Nat.In.intInRange (nat3 |> Nat.N.toIn) (nat12 |> Nat.N.toIn)

9 |> clampBetween3And12 --> Nat 9

0 |> clampBetween3And12 --> Nat 3

99 |> clampBetween3And12 --> Nat 12
```

If you want to handle the cases `< minimum` & `> maximum` explicitly, use [`isIntInRange`](Nat-In#isIntInRange).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min firstMax) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In firstMax max) -> Basics.Int -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max)

values.name: is
values.type: ( Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N tried Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus greaterRange) Nat.Bound.To max)), Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N tried Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference a Nat.Bound.To (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus triedPlusAMinus1))) ) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus lessRange) Nat.Bound.To tried)) } -> { equal : () -> result, less : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min triedPlusAMinus1) -> result, greater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In (Nat.N.Type.Nat2Plus triedMinus1) max) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max) -> result

values.name: isAtLeast
values.comment:
Is the `Nat (In ...)`

  - `equalOrGreater` than a `Nat` or

  - `less`?

```
vote : { age : Nat (In (Nat18Plus orOlder) max) } -> Vote

tryToVote =
    Nat.In.lowerMin (nat0 |> Nat.N.toIn)
        >> Nat.In.isAtLeast ( nat18, nat18 )
            { min = nat0 }
            { less = Nothing --😓
            , equalOrGreater = \age -> Just (vote { age = age })
            }
```
values.type: ( Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N tried Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference a Nat.Bound.To (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus triedMinus1PlusA))), Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N tried Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference atLeastRange Nat.Bound.To max)) ) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus lessRange) Nat.Bound.To tried)) } -> { less : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min triedMinus1PlusA) -> result, equalOrGreater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In tried max) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max) -> result

values.name: isAtMost
values.comment:
Is the `Nat (In ...)`

  - `equalOrLess` than a `Nat` or

  - `greater`?

```
goToU18Party : { age : Nat (In min Nat17) } -> List Snack

tryToGoToU18Party =
    Nat.In.lowerMin (nat0 |> Nat.N.toIn)
        >> Nat.In.isAtMost ( nat17, nat17 )
            { min = nat0 }
            { equalOrLess = \age -> Just (goToU18Party { age 0 age })
            , greater = Nothing
            }
```
values.type: ( Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N tried Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference a Nat.Bound.To triedPlusA)), Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N tried Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus greaterRange) Nat.Bound.To max)) ) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference atMostRange Nat.Bound.To tried)) } -> { equalOrLess : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min triedPlusA) -> result, greater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In tried max) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max) -> result

values.name: isInRange
values.comment:
Compared to a range `first` to `last`, is the `Nat (In ...)`

  - `inRange`

  - `greater` than the `last` or

  - `less` than the `first`?

```
justIfBetween3And10 =
    Nat.In.lowerMin (nat0 |> Nat.N.toIn)
        >> Nat.In.isInRange
            { first = ( nat3, nat3 ), last = ( nat10, nat10 ) }
            { min = nat0 }
            { less = \_ -> Nothing
            , greater = \_ -> Nothing
            , inRange = Just
            }

justIfBetween3And10 (nat9 |> Nat.N.toIn)
--> Just (Nat 9)

justIfBetween3And10 (nat123 |> Nat.N.toIn)
--> Nothing
```
values.type: { first : ( Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N first Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference range Nat.Bound.To last)), Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N first Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference a Nat.Bound.To (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus firstMinus1PlusA))) ), last : ( Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N last Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus greaterRange) Nat.Bound.To max)), Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N last Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference a Nat.Bound.To lastPlusA)) ) } -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus lessRange) Nat.Bound.To first)) } -> { inRange : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In first lastPlusA) -> result, less : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min firstMinus1PlusA) -> result, greater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus last) max) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max) -> result

values.name: isIntInRange
values.comment:
An `Int` compared to a range from `first` to `last`.

    rejectOrAcceptUserInt =
        isIntInRange (nat1 |> Nat.N.toIn) (nat100 |> Nat.N.toIn)
            { less = Err "must be >= 1"
            , greater = \_-> Err "must be <= 100"
            , inRange = Ok
            }

    rejectOrAcceptUserInt (Nat.N.toIn nat0)
    --> Err "must be >= 1"
values.type: { first : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In minFirst last), last : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In last lastPlusA) } -> { less : () -> result, greater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus last)) -> result, inRange : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In minFirst lastPlusA) -> result } -> Basics.Int -> result

values.name: lowerMin
values.comment:
Set the minimum lower.

    [ Nat.N.toIn nat3, Nat.N.toIn nat4 ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are: `Nat (In Nat3 ...)`

    [ Nat.N.toIn nat3
    , Nat.N.toIn nat4 |> Nat.In.lowerMin (nat3 |> Nat.N.toIn)
    ]
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In lowerMin min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In lowerMin max)

values.name: maxIs
values.comment:
Make it fit into functions with higher maximum.

You should design type annotations as general as possible.

    onlyAtMost18 : Nat (In min Nat18)

    onlyAtMost18 between3And8 --fine

But once you implement `onlyAtMost18`, you might use the value in `onlyAtMost19`.

    onlyAtMost18 value =
        -- onlyAtMost19 value --error :(
        onlyAtMost19
            (value |> Nat.In.maxIs nat18 {- works :) -})

[`lowerMin`](Nat-In#lowerMin) is also handy in those situations.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N max Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference a Nat.Bound.To maxPlusA)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min maxPlusA)

values.name: random
values.comment: Generate a random `Nat (In ...)` in a range.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In firstMin lastMin) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In lastMin lastMax) -> Random.Generator (Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In firstMin lastMax))

values.name: range
values.comment:
`Nat (In ...)`s from a first to a last value.

    from3To10 =
        Nat.In.range (nat3 |> Nat.N.toIn) (nat10 |> Nat.N.toIn)

The resulting `List` is never empty.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In firstMin lastMin) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In lastMin lastMax) -> List.List (Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In firstMin lastMax))

values.name: remainderBy
values.comment:
The remainder after division. `remainderBy 0` is impossible.

    Nat.N.toIn nat7 |> Nat.In.remainderBy (nat3 |> Nat.N.toMin)
    --> Nat 1 of type Nat (In Nat0 (Nat7Plus a))
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus dMinMinus1)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In Nat.N.Type.Nat0 max)

values.name: sub
values.comment:
Subtract a `Nat (In ...)`.

The 2 following arguments are

  - the minimum subtracted value
  - the maximum subtracted value

If you don't have both at hand, use [`subLossy`](Nat-In#subLossy).

    nat6 |> Nat.N.toIn
        |> Nat.In.sub between1And5 nat1 nat5
    --> is of type Nat (In Nat1 (Nat5Plus a))
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In subtractedMin subtractedMax) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N subtractedMin Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference differenceMax Nat.Bound.To max)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N subtractedMax Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference differenceMin Nat.Bound.To min)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In differenceMin differenceMax)

values.name: subLossy
values.comment:
Subtract a `Nat (In ..)` safely, but without calculating the new bounds.

    nat6 |> Nat.N.toIn
        |> Nat.In.subLossy between1And5
    --> is of type Nat (In Nat0 (Nat6Plus a))

If you have both

  - the minimum subtracted value
  - the maximum subtracted value

at hand, use [`sub`](Nat-In#sub).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In subtractedMin min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In Nat.N.Type.Nat0 max)

values.name: subN
values.comment:
Subtract a fixed `Nat` value.

    nat7 |> Nat.N.toIn
        |> Nat.In.subN ( nat7, nat7 )
    --> is of type Nat (In Nat0 a)
values.type: ( Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N subtracted Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference differenceMin Nat.Bound.To min)), Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N subtracted Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference differenceMax Nat.Bound.To max)) ) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min max) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In differenceMin differenceMax)

name: Nat.Min

values.name: abs
values.comment:
The absolute value of an `Int`, which is at least `Nat0`.

    Nat.Min.abs 16 --> Nat 16

    Nat.Min.abs -4 --> Nat 4

Really only use this if you want the absolute value.

    badLength list =
        List.length >> Nat.Min.abs

    goodLength =
        List.foldl
            (\_ ->
                Nat.Min.addN nat1
                    >> Nat.Min.lowerMin (nat0 |> Nat.N.toIn)
            )
            (Nat.N.toMin nat0)

If something like this isn't possible, use [`Nat.Min.intAtLeast`](Nat-Min#intAtLeast)!
values.type: Basics.Int -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min Nat.N.Type.Nat0)

values.name: add
values.comment:
Add a `Nat (Min ...)`. The second argument is the minimum if the added `Nat (Min ...)`.

    nat5
        |> Nat.N.toMin
        |> Nat.Min.add atLeast2 nat2
    --> is of type Nat (Min Nat7)

If you have don't the minimum added value at hand, use [`addLossy`](Nat-Min#addLossy).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min addedMin) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N addedMin Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference min Nat.Bound.To sumMin)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min sumMin)

values.name: addLossy
values.comment:
Add a `Nat (Min ...)`. The second argument is the minimum if the added `Nat (Min ...)`.

    atLeast5 |> Nat.Min.addLossy atLeast2
    --> is of type Nat (Min Nat5)

    atLeast2 |> Nat.Min.addLossy atLeast5
    --> is of type Nat (Min Nat2)

If you have the minimum added value at hand, use [`add`](Nat-Min#add).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min addedMin) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min)

values.name: addN
values.comment:
Add an exact `Nat (N ...)`.

    atLeast5 |> Nat.Min.addN nat2
    --> is of type Nat (Min Nat7)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N n Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference min Nat.Bound.To minPlusN)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min minPlusN)

values.name: atLeast
values.comment:
If the `Nat (Min ...)` is lower than a lower limit, return the lower limit instead.

    atLeast5 : Nat (Min min) -> Nat (Min Nat5)
    atLeast5 =
        Nat.Min.atLeast (nat5 |> Nat.N.toMin)

    atLeast5 (nat3 |> Nat.N.toMin) --> Nat 5
    atLeast5 (nat12 |> Nat.N.toMin) --> Nat 12
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min newMin) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min newMin)

values.name: atMost
values.comment:
**Cap** the `Nat (Min ...)` to at most a upper limit.

    atMost5 : Nat (Min min) -> Nat (Min Nat5)
    atMost5 =
        Nat.Min.lowerMin (nat0 |> Nat.N.toIn)
            >> Nat.Min.atMost (nat5 |> Nat.N.toMin) { min = nat0 }

    atMost5 (nat12 |> Nat.N.toMin) --> Nat 5
    atMost5 (nat3 |> Nat.N.toMin) --> Nat 3
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In max maxPlusA) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference range Nat.Bound.To max)) } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min maxPlusA)

values.name: div
values.comment:
Divide (`//`) by a `Nat.Min`. `div 0` is impossible.

    Nat.N.toMin nat7 |> Nat.Min.div (nat3 |> Nat.N.toMin)
    --> Nat 2 of type Nat (Min Nat0)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus minMinus1)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min Nat.N.Type.Nat0)

values.name: intAtLeast
values.comment:
A `Nat (Min ...)` from an `Int`; if the `Int < minimum`, `minimum` is returned.

    9 |> Nat.Min.intAtLeast nat3 --> Nat 9

    0 |> Nat.Min.intAtLeast nat3 --> Nat 3

You can also use this if you know an `Int` must be at least `minimum`.

But avoid it if you can do better, like

    goodLength =
        List.foldl
            (\_ ->
                Nat.Min.addN nat1
                    >> Nat.Min.lowerMin (nat0 |> Nat.N.toIn)
            )
            (Nat.N.toMin nat0)

If you want to handle the case `< minimum` yourself, use [`Nat.Min.isIntAtLeast`](Nat-Min#isIntAtLeast).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> Basics.Int -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min)

values.name: is
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus triedMinus1) (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus triedMinus1PlusA)) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference lessRange Nat.Bound.To triedMinus1)) } -> { equal : () -> result, less : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min triedMinus1PlusA) -> result, greater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min (Nat.N.Type.Nat2Plus triedMinus1)) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> result

values.name: isAtLeast
values.comment:
Is the `Nat (Min ...)`

  - `equalOrGreater` than a `Nat` or

  - `less`?

```
factorial : Nat.Min min -> Nat.Min Nat1
factorial =
    Nat.Min.lowerMin (nat0 |> Nat.N.toIn)
        >> Nat.Min.isAtLeast (nat1 |> Nat.N.toIn)
            { min = nat0 }
            { less = \_ -> nat1 |> Nat.N.toMin
            , equalOrGreater =
                \atLeast1 ->
                    atLeast1
                        |> Nat.Min.mul
                            (factorial
                                (atLeast1 |> Nat.Min.subN nat1)
                            )
            }
```
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In triedMin (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus triedMinMinus1PlusA)) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference lessRange Nat.Bound.To triedMin)) } -> { less : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min triedMinMinus1PlusA) -> result, equalOrGreater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min triedMin) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> result

values.name: isAtMost
values.comment:
Is the `Nat (Min ...)`

  - `equalOrLess` than a `Nat` or

  - `greater`?

```
goToU18Party : { age : Nat (In min Nat17) } -> List Snack

tryToGoToU18Party =
    Nat.Min.lowerMin (nat0 |> Nat.N.toIn)
        >> Nat.Min.isAtMost ( nat17, nat17 )
            { min = nat0 }
            { equalOrLess = \age -> Just (goToU18Party { age = age })
            , greater = Nothing
            }
```
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In atMostMin atMostMinPlusA) -> { min : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N min Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference minToAtMostMin Nat.Bound.To atMostMin)) } -> { equalOrLess : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In min atMostMinPlusA) -> result, greater : Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min atMostMin) -> result } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> result

values.name: isIntAtLeast
values.comment:
If the `Int >= a minimum`, `Just` the `Nat (Min minimum)`, else `Nothing`.

    4 |> Nat.Min.isIntAtLeast nat5 --> Nothing

    1234 |> Nat.Min.isIntAtLeast nat5 --> Just (Nat 1234)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> Basics.Int -> Maybe.Maybe (Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min))

values.name: lowerMin
values.comment:
Set the minimum lower.

    [ Nat.N.toMin nat3, Nat.N.toMin nat4 ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are: `Nat (Min Nat3)`

    [ Nat.N.toMin nat3
    , Nat.N.toMin nat4 |> Nat.Min.lowerMin (nat3 |> Nat.N.toIn)
    ]
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In lowerMin min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min lowerMin)

values.name: mul
values.comment:
Multiply by a `Nat (Min ...)` >= 1.
we know that if `a >= 1  →  x * a >= x`

    five |> Nat.Min.mul two
    --> Nat 10 of type Nat (Min Nat5)

    two |> Nat.Min.mul five
    --> Nat 10 of type Nat (Min Nat2)

    two = nat2 |> Nat.N.toMin

    five = nat5 |> Nat.N.toMin
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus multipliedMinMinus1)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min)

values.name: range
values.comment:
`Nat (Min ...)`s from a first `Nat (In ...)` to a last `Nat (Min ...)`.

An empty range cannot be constructed.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In firstMin lastMin) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min lastMin) -> List.List (Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min firstMin))

values.name: remainderBy
values.comment:
The remainder after division. `remainderBy 0` is impossible.

    Nat.N.toMin nat7 |> Nat.Min.remainderBy (Nat.N.toMin nat3)
    --> Nat 1 of type Nat (Min Nat0)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus dMinMinus1)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min Nat.N.Type.Nat0)

values.name: subIn
values.comment:
Subtract a `Nat (In ...)`. The second argument is the maximum if the subtracted `Nat (In ...)`.

    atLeast6 |> Nat.Min.subIn inNat0To5 nat5
    --> is of type Nat (Min Nat1)

If you have don't the maximum subtracted value at hand, use [`subInLossy`](Nat-Min#subInLossy).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In subtractedMin subtractedMax) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N subtractedMax Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference differenceMin Nat.Bound.To min)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min differenceMin)

values.name: subInLossy
values.comment:
Subtract a `Nat (In ..)` safely, but without calculating the new minimum.

    nat6 |> Nat.N.toMin
        |> Nat.In.subInLossy between1And5
    --> is of type Nat (Min Nat0)

If you have the maximum subtracted value at hand, use [`sub`](Nat-Min#sub).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In subtractedMin min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min Nat.N.Type.Nat0)

values.name: subN
values.comment:
Subtract an exact `Nat`.

    (nat7 |> Nat.N.toMin)
        |> Nat.Min.subN nat2
    --> is of type Nat (Min Nat5)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N n Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference minMinusN Nat.Bound.To min)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min minMinusN)

values.name: toPower
values.comment:
The `Nat (Min ...) ^ a Nat (Min ...)`.
We know that if `a >= 1  →  x ^ a >= x`

    five |> Nat.Min.toPower two
    --> Nat 25 of type Nat (Min Nat5)

    two |> Nat.Min.toPower five
    --> Nat 25 of type Nat (Min Nat2)

    two = nat2 |> Nat.N.toMin

    five = nat5 |> Nat.N.toMin
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus minMinus1)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min min)

name: Nat.N

values.name: add1
values.comment:
The `Nat (N ...)` plus 1.

Keep in mind that if you want add _any_ number,
rather go for [`Nat (In ...)`](Nat-In) or [`Nat (Min ...)`](Nat-Min).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N n Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference a Nat.Bound.To nPlusA)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus n) Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference a Nat.Bound.To (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus nPlusA)))

values.name: sub1
values.comment:
The `Nat (N ...)` minus 1.

Keep in mind that if you want subtract _any_ number,
rather go for [`Nat (In ...)`](Nat-Bound#In) or [`Nat (Min ...)`](Nat-Bound#Min).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus nMinus1) Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference a Nat.Bound.To (Nat.N.Type.Nat1Plus nMinus1PlusA))) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N nMinus1 Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference a Nat.Bound.To nMinus1PlusA))

values.name: toIn
values.comment:
A `Nat (In ...)` from an exact `Nat (N n ...)`.

The minimum is `n`, the maximum `>= n`.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N n Nat.Bound.Is (Nat.Bound.Difference a Nat.Bound.To nPlusA)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.In n nPlusA)

values.name: toMin
values.comment:
Convert an exact `Nat n` to a `Nat (Min n)`.

    nat4 |> Nat.N.toMin
    --> is of type Nat (Min Nat4)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.N n Nat.Bound.Is difference) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Bound.Min n)

name: Nat.N.Type
